title: DNA Extraction from FANS sorted nuclei
author: Stefania S Policicchio
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bmpmk5k6

steps.title: Defrost frozen FANS sorted nuclei contained in a 1
steps.instruction: Defrost frozen FANS sorted nuclei contained in a 1.5ml eppendorf tube On ice NOTE 1-2 nuclei aliquots (~200,000 nuclei/tube) per each nuclei population should yield between 300-500ng DNA).

steps.title: To each sample add either 250µL...
steps.instruction: To each sample add either 250µL / 500µL/ 1mL of Slagboom buffer (SB) depending on size of cell pellet. Normally 250µL is sufficient for 200,000 nuclei aliquot or 1x10 6 cells

steps.title: Add 500µL of SB to each...
steps.instruction: Add 500µL of SB to each nuclei sample (if nuclei pellet)* * If nuclei are stored in running buffer: calculate the volume of 10x STE and 5% SDS to add to each sample to be consistent with the composition of SB (1x STE; 0.5% SDS). e.g. If you have collected a 400µL sample you would add 50µL 10x STE and 50µL 5% SDS to make the sample up to 500µL

steps.title: Add 1µL of RNase A per 500uL buffer
steps.instruction: Add 1µL of RNase A per 500uL buffer

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:45:00 (using a heat block)
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:45:00 (using a heat block)

steps.title: Add proteinase K to a final concentration 2mg/mL (e
steps.instruction: Add proteinase K to a final concentration 2mg/mL (e.g 5µL for every 500µL of SB)

steps.title: Mix by inverting 10 times (do...
steps.instruction: Mix by inverting 10 times (do not vortex or pipette mix)

steps.title: Incubate at 60 °C for 01:00:00 (water bath)
steps.instruction: Incubate at 60 °C for 01:00:00 (water bath)

steps.title: Move samples from the water bath...
steps.instruction: Move samples from the water bath and leave at Room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: Briefly spin down tubes (pulse spin)
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down tubes (pulse spin)

steps.title: For every 500µL of SB used,...
steps.instruction: For every 500µL of SB used, add 100 µL of Majiik Mix (e.g. 200 µL for 1 mL of SB)

steps.title: Mix by vigorous inversions ( DO NOT vortex)
steps.instruction: Mix by vigorous inversions ( DO NOT vortex)

steps.title: Centrifuge at 17000 x g, °C,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 17000 x g, °C, for 00:10:00 at Room temperature

steps.title: Carefully recover supernatant and transfer it...
steps.instruction: Carefully recover supernatant and transfer it to a new labelled 1.5ml tube (leaving ~50µL at the bottom of the tube)

steps.title: Repeat step above by adding another...
steps.instruction: Repeat step above by adding another 100µL of Majiik Mix to the new tube

steps.title: Mix by vigorous inversions ( DO NOT vortex)
steps.instruction: Mix by vigorous inversions ( DO NOT vortex)

steps.title: Centrifuge at 17000 x g, °C, for 00:10:00 Room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 17000 x g, °C, for 00:10:00 Room temperature

steps.title: Recover supernatant and transfer to a new labelled 1
steps.instruction: Recover supernatant and transfer to a new labelled 1.5ml tube leaving ~50ul at the bottom of the old tube. NOTE - If supernatant exceeds 1mL, split into 2 tubes

steps.title: Add an equal volume of 100%...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of 100% Isopropanol to each tube (e.g. 1mL supernatant + 1 mL 100% Isopropanol)

steps.title: After adding the Isopropanol, add 0
steps.instruction: After adding the Isopropanol, add 0.7-1µL Glycogen Blue per tube (this step is optional – but advised as it helps to visualize DNA pellet at the end)

steps.title: Mix by inversion (10 times)
steps.instruction: Mix by inversion (10 times)

steps.title: Centrifuge at 17000 x g, °C,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 17000 x g, °C, for 00:15:00 at Room temperature (hinges of tubes facing upwards)

steps.title: Carefully remove supernatant and discard NOTE...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove supernatant and discard NOTE - Care must be taken when pouring supernatant since the DNA pellet only weakly adheres to the side of the tube

steps.title: Proceed by adding 500µL of 80% ethanol to each tube
steps.instruction: Proceed by adding 500µL of 80% ethanol to each tube

steps.title: Mix gently by pipetting
steps.instruction: Mix gently by pipetting

steps.title: Centrifuge at 17000 x g, °C,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 17000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 at Room temperature

steps.title: Carefully discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully discard supernatant

steps.title: Pulse centrifuge to collect remaining liquid...
steps.instruction: Pulse centrifuge to collect remaining liquid at the bottom of the tube

steps.title: Recover and discard residual liquid from...
steps.instruction: Recover and discard residual liquid from the bottom of the tube making sure not to disturb the DNA pellet

steps.title: Leave DNA pellets to air dry...
steps.instruction: Leave DNA pellets to air dry for 5-15 minutes (leave lids open) OPTIONAL - Leave to dry for additional 00:10:00 at 37 °C but be careful not to over-dry pellets

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 20uL of ddH...
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 20uL of ddH 2 O or 1xTE (15µL per tube if expected very low yield). Avoid pipette mixing, only gently flicking

steps.title: Pulse spin tubes to help pellet...
steps.instruction: Pulse spin tubes to help pellet resuspend/ dislodge from the tube wall

steps.title: Leave tubes at 4 °C overnight...
steps.instruction: Leave tubes at 4 °C overnight to fully resuspend before quantifying DNA samples (Nanodrop or Qubit measurements).
